prompt: It is 2026. kolm.ai sells the best-in-class AI-agent security attestation: a vendor uploads agent logs and gets an Ed25519-signed, offline-verifiable evidence report with a scored red-team battery, mapped to SOC2/ISO42001/NIST AI RMF/EU AI Act/OWASP LLM/MITRE ATLAS, plus a transparency log, multi-framework export (Drata/Vanta/Excel), continuous re-attestation, and named co-signer tiers. To be the UNDISPUTED best in the world with NO further iterations needed, what is the complete state-of-the-art feature/capability set such a product MUST have in 2026? Enumerate exhaustively (product, cryptography, integrations, continuous monitoring, agent-identity, reporting/UX, buyer trust) and for each say whether it is table-stakes or a differentiator. Cite the latest standards/products (with URLs/@handle+date). End with the specific gaps kolm most likely still has versus this ultimate spec.
content:
**State-of-the-art (SOTA) AI-agent security attestation in 2026 requires a comprehensive, multi-layered capability set** that goes well beyond basic log uploads and static red-teaming. It must deliver cryptographically verifiable, continuously updated evidence of security posture, identity integrity, behavioral compliance, and regulatory alignment—while remaining practical for vendors, auditors, and enterprise buyers.[[1]](https://labs.cloudsecurityalliance.org/research/csa-research-note-nist-ai-agent-standards-federal-framework/)[[2]](https://beam.ai/agentic-insights/ai-agent-security-in-2026-the-risks-most-enterprises-still-ignore)

The following enumerates the **complete SOTA feature/capability set**, grouped by the requested categories. Items are classified as **table-stakes** (expected baseline for any credible 2026 product; absence is a disqualifier) or **differentiator** (provides clear competitive edge toward being "undisputed best," often involving advanced tech, depth, automation, or ecosystem leadership). Classifications draw from 2025–2026 enterprise guidance, NIST/CISA agentic frameworks, OWASP updates, and vendor/platform capabilities.

Citations reference key 2025–2026 sources (standards, frameworks, and reports dated where available).

### Product (Core Attestation & Testing Capabilities)
- Automated red-teaming battery with scored results mapped to SOC 2, ISO 42001, NIST AI RMF (including 2026 agent profiles), EU AI Act (high-risk conformity/log requirements), OWASP LLM Top 10 (2025 edition), MITRE ATLAS (agentic extensions), and OWASP Agentic/GenAI risks: **Table-stakes**.[[3]](https://iternal.ai/ai-agent-security-checklist)[[4]](https://owasp.org/www-project-top-10-for-large-language-model-applications/)
- Support for agentic/multi-agent simulations (tool abuse, privilege escalation, lateral movement, memory poisoning, multi-agent coordination attacks): **Differentiator** (most tools still focus on single LLMs).[[5]](https://www.fdd.org/analysis/2026/03/09/regarding-security-considerations-for-artificial-intelligence-agents/)
- SBOM integration + third-party component verification/registry (CISA 2025 SBOM minimum elements): **Table-stakes** for supply-chain risk.[[6]](https://media.defense.gov/2026/Apr/30/2003922823/-1/-1/0/CAREFUL%20ADOPTION%20OF%20AGENTIC%20AI%20SERVICES_FINAL.PDF)
- Sandboxed or isolated execution environments for safe testing + runtime validation: **Differentiator**.
- Pre- and post-deployment adversarial testing with NIST AgentDojo-style methodologies: **Table-stakes**.[[1]](https://labs.cloudsecurityalliance.org/research/csa-research-note-nist-ai-agent-standards-federal-framework/)

### Cryptography
- Ed25519 (or equivalent modern ECC) signatures for offline-verifiable reports: **Table-stakes**.
- Hybrid post-quantum cryptography (NIST-standardized ML-DSA, SLH-DSA signatures + ML-KEM; crypto-agility for future algorithms): **Differentiator** (Ed25519 is not quantum-resistant; long-lived attestations require PQC migration).[[7]](https://csrc.nist.gov/projects/post-quantum-cryptography)[[8]](https://hedera.com/blog/are-ed25519-keys-quantum-resistant-exploring-the-future-of-cryptography/)
- Hardware-backed/remote attestation (TPM 2.0, TEEs like Intel TDX/AMD SNP, Entity Attestation Tokens/EAT for AI agents): **Differentiator** (runtime integrity proofs beyond logs).[[9]](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-messous-eat-ai/00/)[[10]](https://arxiv.org/html/2603.05786v1)
- Verifiable Credentials (W3C VC Data Model) + delegation chains for agent actions: **Differentiator**.[[11]](https://indicio.tech/blog/why-verifiable-credentials-will-power-ai-in-2026/)[[12]](https://fluxapay.xyz/learning/ai-agent-identity-the-key-to-autonomous-commerce)
- Transparency/receipt logs (signed, tamper-evident public or permissioned logs for action receipts, akin to Certificate Transparency): **Differentiator**.[[13]](https://arxiv.org/html/2606.04193v1)

### Integrations
- Exports to Drata, Vanta, Excel, and common GRC/compliance automation platforms: **Table-stakes**.
- Native/runtime integrations with enterprise IAM (just-in-time scoped credentials, zero-trust policies for agents as distinct non-human principals), agent platforms, and observability tools: **Differentiator**.[[14]](https://aembit.io/blog/5-security-considerations-for-managing-ai-agents-and-their-identities/)[[15]](https://agatsoftware.com/blog/ai-agent-security-enterprise-2026/)
- CI/CD pipeline embedding (continuous testing as part of deployment gates): **Table-stakes** for modern DevSecOps.
- Direct feeds into post-market monitoring systems (EU AI Act Art. 72 requirements): **Differentiator**.

### Continuous Monitoring
- Periodic/scheduled re-attestation on log uploads or schedule: **Table-stakes**.
- Real-time or near-real-time behavioral monitoring, drift detection, anomaly alerting, and automated re-testing on changes (e.g., model updates, tool additions): **Differentiator**.[[2]](https://beam.ai/agentic-insights/ai-agent-security-in-2026-the-risks-most-enterprises-still-ignore)[[15]](https://agatsoftware.com/blog/ai-agent-security-enterprise-2026/)
- Immutable audit trails with full attribution (triggers, inputs, decisions, actions, delegation chains): **Table-stakes**.[[2]](https://beam.ai/agentic-insights/ai-agent-security-in-2026-the-risks-most-enterprises-still-ignore)
- Fleet-wide agent discovery/inventory + runtime visibility across environments: **Differentiator**.[[15]](https://agatsoftware.com/blog/ai-agent-security-enterprise-2026/)

### Agent-Identity
- Unique, attributable agent identities (distinct from human/service accounts): **Table-stakes**.[[1]](https://labs.cloudsecurityalliance.org/research/csa-research-note-nist-ai-agent-standards-federal-framework/)
- Attestation-based authentication (runtime environment + posture verification, no static secrets): **Differentiator**.[[14]](https://aembit.io/blog/5-security-considerations-for-managing-ai-agents-and-their-identities/)
- Scoped, just-in-time, least-privilege permissions with bounded delegation chains and human-in-the-loop triggers for high-stakes actions: **Table-stakes** (NIST/CISA emphasis).[[1]](https://labs.cloudsecurityalliance.org/research/csa-research-note-nist-ai-agent-standards-federal-framework/)[[2]](https://beam.ai/agentic-insights/ai-agent-security-in-2026-the-risks-most-enterprises-still-ignore)
- Cryptographic proof of origin, integrity, and policy compliance (e.g., via VCs or EAT): **Differentiator**.

### Reporting/UX
- Scored reports with framework mappings, evidence bundles, and offline verification: **Table-stakes**.
- Multi-format exports (Drata/Vanta/Excel) + transparency log: **Table-stakes**.
- Interactive dashboards, visual attack paths/mitigation recommendations, multi-stakeholder views (security, compliance, exec), and machine-readable + human-readable outputs: **Differentiator**.
- Verifiable credentials or signed receipts for downstream consumption (e.g., by buyers or regulators): **Differentiator**.[[11]](https://indicio.tech/blog/why-verifiable-credentials-will-power-ai-in-2026/)

### Buyer Trust
- SOC 2 Type II (or equivalent) for the attestation service itself: **Table-stakes**.
- Named co-signer tiers (e.g., trusted auditors, standards bodies, or oracles): **Differentiator**.
- Public or permissioned transparency logs + third-party audit attestations: **Differentiator**.
- Formal methods elements, bug bounties on the attestation process, or quantum-resistant signatures: **Differentiator**.
- Alignment with emerging agent-specific guidance (e.g., NCCoE agent identity concept paper, CISA agentic AI guidance): **Table-stakes** for credibility.[[1]](https://labs.cloudsecurityalliance.org/research/csa-research-note-nist-ai-agent-standards-federal-framework/)

**Key latest standards/products cited** (with URLs/@handle+date where available from results):
- NIST AI RMF (incl. 2026 agent profiles/concept notes): nist.gov/itl/ai-risk-management-framework (April 2026 updates).[[16]](https://www.nist.gov/itl/ai-risk-management-framework)
- ISO/IEC 42001:2023 (AI Management System, certifiable): Widely referenced for conformity with EU AI Act.
- EU AI Act (high-risk obligations effective ~Aug 2026: risk mgmt, logging 6+ months, conformity assessment): salt.security/eu-ai-act-compliance.
- OWASP LLM Top 10 (2025 edition): genai.owasp.org/llm-top-10/ (Nov 2024/2025 updates, incl. new risks like system prompt leakage).[[4]](https://owasp.org/www-project-top-10-for-large-language-model-applications/)
- MITRE ATLAS (agentic extensions): atlas.mitre.org/ (ongoing, covers agent tactics).[[17]](https://atlas.mitre.org/)
- NIST PQC standards (ML-DSA, SLH-DSA, ML-KEM): csrc.nist.gov/projects/post-quantum-cryptography (finalized/ongoing 2024–2026).
- W3C Verifiable Credentials Data Model: For agent identity/delegation.
- CISA/NCCoE agent identity guidance (Feb 2026 concept paper).[[1]](https://labs.cloudsecurityalliance.org/research/csa-research-note-nist-ai-agent-standards-federal-framework/)

### Specific Gaps kolm.ai Most Likely Still Has vs. This Ultimate Spec (as of mid-2026)
kolm.ai’s described offering (log uploads → Ed25519-signed scored red-team reports with mappings, transparency log, multi-framework export, continuous re-attestation, co-signer tiers) is already strong on core reporting and compliance mapping. However, probable gaps against the full SOTA include:
- Limited or no native support for **hardware/remote attestation** (TPM/TEE/EAT) or runtime environment proofs—relying primarily on logs.
- Absence of **post-quantum/hybrid crypto** and full crypto-agility (critical differentiator for long-lived evidence).
- Insufficient depth in **multi-agent simulations**, runtime behavioral monitoring/drift detection, and fleet-wide discovery (vs. pure log-based re-attestation).
- Lack of **Verifiable Credentials**, delegation chains, or advanced transparency/receipt logs for buyer-side verification.
- Weaker **agent-native IAM integrations** (just-in-time, zero-trust, non-human principals) and sandboxed execution testing.
- Possibly missing formal methods, ZK elements, or public transparency log features that build maximal buyer trust.

Closing these (especially PQC, hardware attestation, runtime continuity, and VCs) would position it as truly undisputed. Many gaps align with 2026 enterprise and regulatory emphasis on treating agents as first-class, attestable identities with continuous assurance.[[14]](https://aembit.io/blog/5-security-considerations-for-managing-ai-agents-and-their-identities/)[[15]](https://agatsoftware.com/blog/ai-agent-security-enterprise-2026/)

This spec represents the converged expectations from NIST, CISA, OWASP, MITRE, ISO, and leading platforms in 2026.
